markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define import behavior from markdown-like notes if needed while rejecting markdown vault as authority. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: ObsidianImportBoundary leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ObsidianImportBoundary is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Markdown-like import boundary: import creates LoomBlock + RichDocument authority rows in PostgreSQL; the markdown SOURCE / vault layout is NEVER authority. THE anti-Obsidian-clone boundary (Master Spec 10.12 #9.1.1 + MT-187).
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - impl: import_snippet(markdown) -> create_knowledge_rich_document (authority) -> create_loom_block(note) -> bridge_loom_block_to_knowledge; document_id left NULL (loom_blocks.document_id FKs the legacy documents table, so the RichDocument link is logical via rich_document_id)

evidence.result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed (real PostgreSQL). Includes the OPERATOR HARD REQUIREMENT negative test.
tests: markdown_import_creates_authority_never_vault - import yields a real authority LoomBlock backed by a real RichDocument (KRD-...), bridged to the ProjectKnowledgeIndex (KEN-...); round-trips from PG; the markdown source is never stored as authority

deleting_projection_leaves_block_authority_byte_identical (OPERATOR HARD REQ) - compile a wiki projection from a bridged block, delete the projection, prove the LoomBlock authority is BYTE-IDENTICAL (serde fingerprint) AND the ProjectKnowledgeIndex bridge (entity_id + index_event_id) is unchanged

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 043cf8c8.

findings: VAULT-AS-AUTHORITY (handled+tested): the markdown source text is parsed and discarded; only the RichDocument (parsed block tree) + LoomBlock + ProjectKnowledgeIndex bridge are persisted. No markdown/file-path is stored as authority.

PROJECTION-DELETE-MUTATES-AUTHORITY (handled+tested, OPERATOR HARD REQ): a serde fingerprint of the block before/after a projection delete is byte-identical, and the bridge entity+receipt are unchanged; knowledge_wiki_projections carries no authority FK.

CROSS-TABLE FK (fixed during impl): loom_blocks.document_id FKs the legacy documents table, not knowledge_rich_documents; setting it to a KRD id violated the FK. Fixed by leaving document_id NULL and carrying the RichDocument link logically in LoomMarkdownImport.rich_document_id.
IMPORT WARNINGS SURFACED (handled): unsupported markdown features come back as warnings (code:detail) rather than silently dropped.
FAIL-CLOSED (handled): a blank title is rejected; the created note is bridged (authority-resolved) before success is reported.
fixes: Leave loom_blocks.document_id NULL on import (RichDocument link is logical) to avoid the legacy-documents FK violation.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-187 (ObsidianImportBoundary) requires a markdown-like import that creates Handshake-native PostgreSQL authority rows while rejecting markdown/vault layout as the source of truth. The code delivers exactly this: import_markdown_to_loom parses markdown via the real MT-151 importer, creates an authority RichDocument + bridged LoomBlock note in real PostgreSQL, and never stores the markdown source as authority; proof runs against real managed PG.
findings: postgres.rs:10619 import_markdown_to_loom is a real implementation: validates title fail-closed, calls import_snippet(markdown, Markdown) to parse to a ProseMirror tree, creates an authority RichDocument via create_knowledge_rich_document (KRD- id), creates a LoomBlock note via create_loom_block, then bridges it to ProjectKnowledgeIndex+EventLedger via bridge_loom_block_to_knowledge. The markdown source text is discarded after parsing - only PostgreSQL authority rows persist. This is the anti-vault boundary the MT requires.

The NotImplemented returns in storage/mod.rs:2297+ are the ABSTRACT trait DEFAULTS, not on the live path. The concrete PostgresDatabase impl overrides import_markdown_to_loom with the working body, so sub_rule_1 has no deferred-live escape reachable from the required behavior.

import.rs:57 import_snippet is a real deterministic markdown parser (MT-151): handles ATX headings, blockquotes, fenced code, lists, paragraphs; unsupported features become repairable importedRaw nodes with typed warnings rather than silent drops. Not a stub.

api/loom.rs:1228 import_markdown_to_loom HTTP handler is wired end-to-end: route /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/import/markdown (loom.rs:190), ensures workspace, calls real state.storage.import_markdown_to_loom, and records a FlightRecorder LoomBlockCreated event. Real IPC/route wiring, not orphaned.

tests/loom_wiki_boundary_tests.rs:224 markdown_import_creates_authority_never_vault exercises real PostgreSQL via pg_or_skip!/knowledge_pg(); asserts content_type=Note, rich_document_id starts with KRD- (real RichDocument), bridge entity_id starts with KEN- (real ProjectKnowledgeIndex resolution), and the block round-trips from PG with the imported title.

tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:108-156 knowledge_pg() connects to real PostgreSQL via POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL, creates an isolated schema, and runs the full migration chain (run_migrations). Satisfies sub_rule_2 real Handshake-owned resource touch; no SQLite/in-memory/mock-only path.

No SQLite path: grep of src/ shows only comments explicitly stating SQLite is never used; sqlx has no sqlite feature per the status note. Forbidden-store constraints (DEC-008, vault-as-authority) are honored.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 10-product-surfaces.md:8534 (Section 10.12) requires the WP-KERNEL-009 surface to be 'a projection over product authority, not a second store' - the impl enforces this by making the parsed PostgreSQL rows authoritative and the markdown source non-authoritative.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): MT contract cites spec anchor §10.12 #9.1.1 and packet cites v02.192; current spec is v02.194. Paperwork pointer staleness only, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
